title: Network Partition Between Availability Zones Causing Split-Brain
symptoms: Network connectivity loss between AZ-a and AZ-b subnets
Database cluster showing two active primary nodes simultaneously
Application servers in AZ-b unable to reach services in AZ-a
Consensus layer triggering automatic failover in isolated AZ
Conflicting write operations detected after partition healed
root_cause: AWS inter-AZ network fabric degradation caused complete connectivity loss between us-east-1a and us-east-1b, and the consensus algorithm had insufficient quorum requirements to prevent split-brain
resolution: Partition self-healed after 12 minutes, manual split-brain resolution performed by identifying and merging conflicting records, consensus algorithm updated to require majority quorum across all AZs
preventive_actions: Update consensus algorithm to require strict majority quorum across AZs
Implement fencing tokens to prevent split-brain write conflicts
Add network partition detection with automatic write-lock on minority partition
Create data reconciliation runbook for split-brain scenarios
Deploy cross-AZ network monitoring with partition detection alerts

timeline.event: Network partition detected between us-east-1a and us-east-1b
Consensus layer in AZ-b promotes replica to primary
Split-brain condition begins with two active primaries
Alerts fire for network partition and dual-primary detection
Maria Garcia assesses situation, confirms partition
Decision to wait for partition healing rather than force intervention
Original primary restored as sole primary, reconciliation begins

embedding_summary: Network partition between AWS Availability Zones caused split-brain condition with two active database primaries writing conflicting data for 12 minutes. 847 conflicting records required manual reconciliation. Resolved by merging conflicts and updating consensus algorithm to require strict majority quorum.
